**Acceptance Criteria**

Blocking failure: REQ-01 and REQ-02 are not met as written. `jit validate` only reports enforcement drift when a repository authors an opt-in `enforcement-drift` rule. The implementation explicitly documents that no built-in default rule declares it, so repos without that rule “never see drift findings from `jit validate`” in [rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:698). The validate path injects drift inputs, but only evaluated graph rules consume them in [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:510) and [graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:546). I also found no live `.jit/rules.toml` `enforcement-drift` rule. The hard criteria say “Validate reports” both drift directions, not “Validate reports when an opt-in rule is configured.”

REQ-03 is covered by unit/graph/CLI tests, including [drift.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/drift.rs:251), [graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:4111), and [invariant_check_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/invariant_check_cli_tests.rs:40). REQ-04 through REQ-07 look implemented: render delegates through [invariant.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/invariant.rs:128), projection writes through storage in [projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/projection.rs:247), both CLI subcommands have `--json` in [cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:409), check exits non-zero on drift in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:575), and the stale checker-command comment is corrected in [local.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/local.rs:289).

**Architecture And Separation Of Concerns**

No direct file persistence outside storage was found for the new render path. `project_invariants` uses `IssueStore::read_repo_file` / `write_repo_file` in [projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/projection.rs:258), and `JsonFileStorage::write_repo_file` performs the atomic write in [json.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:804). CLI output remains in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:533), with command methods returning structured results.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking issue found. The drift core is pure and iterator-based in [drift.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/drift.rs:200), and graph wrapping uses iterator mapping in [graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:562).

**Correctness And Invariants**

Blocking correctness issue is the validate-contract gap above: a default `jit validate` run can pass while drift exists because enforcement drift is opt-in. Atomic writes for projection are preserved through storage; no event logging, gate transition, assignee/label format, PID safety, or unsafe-code regressions were found in the reviewed paths.

**Safety**

No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed implementation. Workspace `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains compatible with this change.

**Testing**

Per instruction, I did not rerun cargo tests because the issue context has a passed `cargo-ci` gate at `2026-06-25T22:22:52Z`. I did run `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed.

Coverage exists for `jit invariant check`, render projection, and graph rule dispatch. The missing coverage is an end-to-end `jit validate` test proving drift is reported without requiring an extra opt-in rule, which is the behavior the hard criteria require.

**Error Handling**

No blocking issue found. Projection errors are typed with contextual messages in [projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/projection.rs:42), and invariant command errors are converted to JSON when requested in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:515).

**CLI Contract**

`jit invariant render --json` and `jit invariant check --json` are wired in [cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:409), and `check --json` emits output before exiting with validation failure code on drift in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:551).

**Documentation**

No blocking documentation issue found for the new public APIs I checked. Public drift/result/projection APIs include doc comments and examples, e.g. [drift.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/drift.rs:30) and [invariant.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/invariant.rs:19).

**Prior Review Feedback**

`run_history` is empty in the provided context, so there is no prior review feedback to verify.

**Jit Issue Management**

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed. The listed dependency `0d593d90` is marked `done` in the issue context, so prerequisite work is complete.

VERDICT: FAIL
